Package org.rodinp.core

Interface IParent

All Known Subinterfaces:

Action, Axiom, CarrierSet, ComponentRoot, Constant, ContextRoot, ContextRoot, Data, Dependency, Event, EventBElement, EventBPOElement, EventBProofElement, EventBRoot, ExtendsContext, Guard, ImplicitHolder, InternalElement, Invariant, Language, MachineRoot, MachineRoot, NamedElement, NamedElement2, Parameter, POIdentifier, POPredicate, POPredicateSet, PORoot, PORoot, POSelectionHint, POSequent, POSource, PRExprRef, PRHypAction, PRIdentifier, PRPredRef, PRProof, PRProofRule, PRReasoner, PRRoot, PRRuleAntecedent, PRStoredExpr, PRStoredPred, PRStringInput, PSRoot, PSStatus, Reference, RefinesEvent, RefinesMachine, RodinDB, RodinFile, RodinProject, RodinTestDependency, RodinTestRoot, RodinTestRoot, RodinTestRoot2, SCAction, SCAssignmentElement, SCAxiom, SCCarrierSet, SCConstant, SCContextRoot, SCContextRoot, SCEvent, SCExpressionElement, SCExtendsContext, SCGuard, SCIdentifierElement, SCInternalContext, SCInvariant, SCMachineRoot, SCMachineRoot, SCParameter, SCPredicateElement, SCRefinesEvent, SCRefinesMachine, SCSeesContext, SCVariable, SCVariant, SCWitness, SeesContext, TestChildElement, TestParentElement, UbiquitousElement, Variable, Variant, VersionEA, VersionEB, VersionEC, VersionRootA, VersionRootB, VersionRootC, VersionRootD, VersionRootE, VersionRootF, VersionRootG, VersionRootH, Witness
public interface IParent
Common protocol for Rodin elements that contain other Rodin elements.
Since:
1.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    IRodinElement[]
    getChildren()
    Returns the immediate children of this element.
    <T extends IRodinElement>
    T[]
    getChildrenOfType(IElementType<T> type)
    Returns the immediate children of this element that are of the given type.
    boolean
    hasChildren()
    Returns whether this element may have one or more immediate children.

  • Method Details

    • getChildren

      IRodinElement[] getChildren() throws RodinDBException
      Returns the immediate children of this element. Unless otherwise specified by the implementing element, the children are ordered.
      Returns:
      the immediate children of this element
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource

    • getChildrenOfType

      <T extends IRodinElement> T[] getChildrenOfType(IElementType<T> type) throws RodinDBException
      Returns the immediate children of this element that are of the given type. The order on children is maintained while extracting them.

      The actual component type of the returned array is the class that corresponds closest to the given element type. For instance, when passing IRodinProject.ELEMENT_TYPE as type, the returned array can be safely casted to IRodinProject[]. The same applies for contributed element types, where the actual component type of the array is the class that was declared in the extension point.

      Parameters:
      type - type of the children to retrieve
      Returns:
      the immediate children of this element that are of the given type
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist, or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource

    • hasChildren

      boolean hasChildren() throws RodinDBException
      Returns whether this element may have one or more immediate children. This is a convenience method, and may be more efficient than testing whether getChildren is an empty array.

      If this method returns false, one can be sure that this element doesn't have any child. On the contrary, if true is returned, this means that the answer is not known (this element may or may not have children).

      Returns:
      true if this element may have immediate children, false otherwise
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource
